---
title: "Metformin Cmax and Tmax Simulation"
output: github_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(fig.align = "left", out.width = "80%")
source(here::here("R", "01_packages.R"))
source(here::here("R", "02_data.R"))
source(here::here("R", "03_simulation.R"))
source(here::here("R", "04_modeling.R"))
source(here::here("R", "05_bootstrap.R"))
source(here::here("R", "06_plotting.R"))
```

# The Original Pharmacokinetics Table

Source: <https://dailymed.nlm.nih.gov/dailymed/drugInfo.cfm?setid=56d13a1c-b289-4528-b23c-60f5427b4552>

```{r original-table, echo=FALSE, eval=FALSE}
knitr::include_graphics(here::here("data", "raw", "table4.png"))
```


# 1. Libraries and Dataset

The project now loads packages and constructs the summary dataset from reusable scripts.

```{r dataset, echo=TRUE}
pk_summary
```

# 2. Simulate the Population

Simulated individual-level data are generated from the summary table using normal assumptions for eGFR and lognormal assumptions for Cmax and Tmax.

```{r sim-data, echo=TRUE}
dplyr::glimpse(sim_data)
```

# 3. Fit the Parameters to Spline (`k = 4`)

Generalized additive models are used to smooth each PK endpoint over eGFR.

```{r model-grid, echo=TRUE}
dplyr::glimpse(prediction_grid)
```

# 4. Build Plots for Cmax and Tmax

```{r joint-plot, echo=FALSE}
p_joint
```

# 5. Bootstrapped Confidence Bands

```{r boot-preview, echo=TRUE}
head(bootstrap_curves)
```

# 6. Plot the Confidence Bands

```{r boot-cmax, echo=FALSE}
p_boot_Cmax
```

```{r boot-cmax-norm, echo=FALSE}
p_boot_Cmax_norm
```

```{r boot-tmax, echo=FALSE}
p_boot_Tmax
```

# 7. Compare the Three Curves

```{r combined-boot, echo=FALSE}
combined_boot
```
